event: 159 Pioneer St built to house Pioneer Iron Works (foundry)
Building burns; rebuilt
Dustin Yellin purchases 25000 sq ft building at 159 Pioneer St for $3.7M
Sam Trimble leads renovation; 100 windows added; ~$200K initial budget largely from Yellin art sales
Pioneer Works Art Foundation incorporated as 501(c)(3) (EIN 46-1097738); originally named Intercourse
Hurricane Sandy floods ground floor with 5 ft of water
Cultural center reopens under the name Pioneer Works
Second Sundays free monthly open house begins
Janna Levin joins as Founding Director of Sciences
First annual Village Fête spring benefit
FY2017 revenue $6.06M; first year top-officer comp visible in ProPublica summary
Spring + Summer + Fall residency cohorts (Khan / Liu / Rasheed / Syms / Allahyari etc.) hosted
$30 million capital campaign launched (accessibility + observatory)
FY2019 revenue spikes to $14.23M
Eric Shiner appointed first-ever Executive Director (start 6 Jan 2020)
COVID closes the building; 7th annual Village Fête cancelled
Broadcast online magazine launched
Eric Shiner departs Pioneer Works
Tenth-anniversary press cycle; 25000 sq ft / 40 ft ceilings facts re-confirmed
Mara Manus appointed inaugural CEO; Austin Hearst named board chair
Broadcast Print Issue 01 published (with hannah baer / Levin / Park / Phillips)
Eight-month renovation closure begins
Pioneer Works reopens; first ADA-accessible season; new elevator + HVAC + roof
Fall Benefit / OPEN reopening gala (honoring Hearst + Yellin)
Empire State Development announces completion of $12.7M renovation ($2.3M state / $10.4M private)
Mara Manus departs at end of month; Florenz expands to Executive Director
Stephanie Hemshrot appointed COO
Village Fête (David Byrne performance)
Public observatory build-out planned: 16-foot 1890s antique telescope on roof deck
